LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE TO SACRAMENTO IS GRANTED. CAL. CORP. CODE 177 PROVIDES THAT THE PROPER VENUE FOR INVOLUNTARY DISSOLUTION PROCEEDINGS IS THE COUNTY WHERE THE CORPORATION'S PRINCIPAL OFFICE IS LOCATED. THE CURRENT DESIGNATION FILED WITH THE SECRETARY OF STATE CONCLUSIVELY FIXES THE CORPORATION'S PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS FOR PURPOSES OF VENUE. (ROSAS V. SUPERIOR COURT (1994) 25 CAL.APP.4TH 671, 677.) A COMPANY MAY NOT DEFEAT VENUE BASED ON THAT DESIGNATION BY SHOWING THAT ITS PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS IS LOCATED ELSEWHERE. (ID.) THE REQUEST FOR SANCTION IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302/LCN)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-15-2013 Continued To Jan-08-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Mtn To Change Venue
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. KALYAN POKALA, TEL. NO. (619) 992-7552 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DOMINIC V. SIGNOROTTI OF THE BUCHMAN PROVINE BROTHERS SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. (925) 944-9700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC'S Motion To Change Venue to Sacramento is Granted. Cal. Corp. Code 177 provides that the proper venue for involuntary dissolution proceedings is the county where the corporation's principal office is located. The current designation filed with the Secretary of State conclusively fixes the corporation's principal place of business for purposes of venue. (Rosas v. Superior Court (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 671, 677.) A company may not defeat venue based on that designation by showing that its principal place of business is located elsewhere. (Id.) The Request for Sanction is Denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 (302/LCN)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-13-2013 Continued To Oct-30-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff Nicholas Blasgen'S Motion To Reconsider Court Order Granting Defendant'S Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration For Further Argument
Off Calendar Aug-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KALYAN POKALA, ESQ., 619.992.7552, FOR PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS BLASGEN.
DOMINIC SIGNOROTTI, ESQ., 925.944.9700, FOR DEFENDANT REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with slight modification: The petition to compel mediation and arbitration is denied. The operating agreement for Refractive Dialer, LLC on which the petition is based does not contain a binding agreement to arbitrate. (Decl. of Joshua Tillman In Support of Petition to Compel Mediation and Arbitration and Stay Action, filed May 1, 2013, 4, Ex. A.) Section VII (4) is entitled "Mediation and Arbitration of Disputes Among Members." The operative language states that "Any party may commence arbitration of the dispute by sending a written request for arbitration to all other parties to the dispute. The request shall state the nature of the dispute to be resolved by arbitration, and, if all parties to the dispute agree to arbitration, arbitration shall be commenced as soon as practical after such parties receive a copy of the written request." Id. Section VII (4) (emphasis added). The evidence shows that plaintiff's earlier offers to arbitrate in late 2012 were never responded to by Defendant and were withdrawn as rejected. The evidence shows that Plaintiff sent multiple letters to the defendant in November and December 2012 stating the nature of the dispute and expressing a willingness to arbitrate. (See Pokala Suppl. Decl. filed August 1, 2013, 4-6, and Exs. A-C.).) Defendant did not respond to these letters. Eventually Plaintiff wrote a letter in January 2013 stating that he would have no alternative but to commence litigation if he did not receive a response. (Pokala declaration filed August 1, 2013 Exhibit C.) The defendant was unresponsive and Plaintiff filed this lawsuit on or about March 28, 2013. By that point, there was no outstanding offer to arbitrate to which the defendant could simply quote agree close quote examine then seek to compel arbitration under the agreement. Plaintiff noted at the time that Defendant had ignored attempt to arbitrate, that Plaintiff treated the silence as refusal to agree to arbitrate and was forced the file its own law site. (See Brothers Decl. filed May 1, 2013, 4, Ex. A (letters between counsel dated April 29, 2013.)The Court find that all parties to the dispute do not agree to arbitrate and the motion to compel is denied. The Court has no authority to compel mediation. Request for attorney's fees are denied. The Court confirms that the previous order annuitied in Court on June 5, 2013 granting the petition to compel arbitration is vacated. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR7489.

Ntc Of Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Jun-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KALYAN POKALA, ESQ., 619.992.7552, FOR PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS BLASGEN; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling REGARDING DEFENDANT REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC's Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration And Stay Action. The petition to compel arbitration is granted, for good cause shown. No opposition was filed. The action now pending in this court (Case CGC-13-530101) is stayed, pending the outcome of the arbitration. The Court has no authority to compel mediation. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: L. Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
